title: Backlog Task
description: One unit of work on the tasks/ kanban board. Exactly one JSON file per task, named <id>.json, living in a lane directory under tasks/ (backlog | in_progress | blocked | done). The lane IS the status; move lanes with `git mv`. Ordering within a lane is lexical by filename, so the numeric id prefix controls execution order.

id.description: Stable identifier. MUST equal the filename without .json. Convention: a 3-digit ordering prefix + kebab slug, e.g. "001-scaffold-nextjs".

title.description: One-line human summary. Used in commit messages and progress output.

depends_on.description: ids of tasks that must be in tasks/done/ before this task is actionable. The selector will not pick this task until every listed id is done.

skills.description: Claude Code skill names the run should load (via the Skill tool) BEFORE implementing — e.g. "frontend-design", "compose-component". Load only what the task genuinely needs.

model.description: Anthropic model the run uses, passed verbatim to `claude --model`. Aliases ("opus", "sonnet", "haiku", "fable") resolve to the latest of that tier; the `claude-*` forms pin an exact version. Omit to use the default, "opus".

context.description: Optional background the run needs but which is NOT itself a pass/fail check: how to approach the work, pointers to prior decisions (e.g. "resolve the app root from JOURNAL.md"), suggested commands. Keep checks out of here — those go in acceptance.

goal.description: The end state in one tight paragraph. What is true when this task is done. Outcome, not steps.

acceptance.description: The pass/fail contract. EACH item must be verifiable by a real command (or a browser check) whose output the run pastes into the transcript. Prefer concrete, copy-pasteable checks: `bun run build` exits 0; file X exists; curl returns 200. Ambiguous items force the task to be blocked, so make them checkable.

tests.description: Tests the run must AUTHOR (not just run) and leave green before the task can be marked done — e.g. "Add a test asserting GET / returns 200". These are additional verification the run writes into the codebase, beyond the acceptance checks.
